title: Recognising AML with MECOM rearrangement (3q26 abnormality)
learning_objectives: Recognise AML associated with MECOM rearrangements
Understand the adverse prognostic significance of MECOM-rearranged AML
clinical_vignette: A 59-year-old man presents with fatigue, bruising and recurrent infections. Blood tests show anaemia, thrombocytopenia and leukocytosis. Bone marrow examination demonstrates acute myeloid leukaemia with dysplastic megakaryocytes.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Circulating myeloblasts with occasional hypogranular neutrophils.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow with increased blasts, multilineage dysplasia and numerous small hypolobated megakaryocytes.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Acute myeloid immunophenotype without lineage ambiguity.

cytogenetics: Conventional karyotype demonstrates inv(3)(q21q26.2). FISH confirms MECOM rearrangement.
molecular_genetics: NGS shows no NPM1 mutation. FLT3-ITD negative.
integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: AML with MECOM rearrangement associated with inv(3)(q21q26.2).
question: Which statement best reflects the significance of the cytogenetic finding?
options: MECOM rearrangement defines a genetically distinct AML subtype associated with adverse prognosis.
MECOM rearrangement is characteristic of acute promyelocytic leukaemia.
MECOM rearrangement indicates favourable-risk core-binding factor AML.
MECOM rearrangement establishes mixed phenotype acute leukaemia.
MECOM rearrangement is only relevant if NPM1 is mutated.
correct_answer: A. MECOM rearrangement defines a genetically distinct AML subtype associated with adverse prognosis.
explanation: Rearrangements involving MECOM, typically through inv(3)(q21q26.2) or t(3;3)(q21;q26.2), define a specific AML subtype recognised by modern classifications. These cases frequently show dysplastic megakaryocytes and are associated with adverse clinical outcomes.

why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Core-binding factor AML is defined by RUNX1::RUNX1T1 or CBFB::MYH11.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: MECOM rearrangement does not define mixed phenotype acute leukaemia.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: Its diagnostic significance is independent of NPM1 status.

frcpath_pearl: Always examine chromosome 3 abnormalities carefully. inv(3)/t(3;3) with MECOM rearrangement represents a high-risk AML subtype even when blast morphology is otherwise unremarkable.
take_home_message: MECOM rearrangements define an adverse-risk AML subtype that should be recognised promptly through integrated morphology and cytogenetic assessment.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
